methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Amber Bio is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Basem Al-Shayeb and Jacob Borrajo that has raised $26M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 11 comparable companies in Biotech & Health—3 public (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

Only one valuation method proved applicable: stage-progression analysis. Using total capital raised ($26M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from seed to the next financing stage yields an implied valuation of $65M. This reflects market precedent for progression within early-stage biotech financing. Peer-median triangulation was not feasible due to an insufficient narrow peer set—while 16 same-sector comparables exist, fewer than three matched on both stage and valuation tier simultaneously. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median 6.6x across the three public tickers) could not be applied without Amber Bio's revenue disclosure, which is typical for private companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $55M to $75M, with $65M as the median point. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable seed-stage biotech transactions in the immediate peer set and the lack of revenue or profitability data to cross-check against public multiples. The single-method result warrants validation through additional data as the company approaches Series A.
